SEARCH
Package server.dao

Class ReportDAO

java.lang.Object
server.dao.ReportDAO
public class ReportDAO extends Object
Read-only data access object backing the department manager's reports.

Follows the same conventions as the other DAOs: each method opens a short-lived Connection from DBConnection, runs parameterized statements, and maps rows into report DTOs. SQL exceptions are logged and surfaced as a null return rather than propagated, so the controller signals failure through the return value.

Both reports take an optional park filter. When a parkId is supplied it is appended as a parameterized AND ... = ? clause; when it is null the clause is omitted entirely so the query spans the whole region. Building the clause this way (never string-concatenating the value itself) keeps the statement injection-safe whether or not a park is given.

  • Constructor Details

    • ReportDAO

      public ReportDAO()

  • Method Details

    • visitsByType

      public VisitsReportDTO visitsByType(String from, String to, Integer parkId)
      Visits-by-type report: per-category visit counts and average stay lengths over a date range, optionally narrowed to a single park.

      The source is visit v LEFT JOIN reservation r so a visit's category can be read from its reservation when it has one and from the visit's own visit_type when it is a casual walk-in (reservation_id IS NULL) — that is what the COALESCE does. Visits are filtered by DATE(entered_at) falling within [from, to] inclusive.

      visitCount is COUNT(*) over every matching visit, but the average stay is AVG(TIMESTAMPDIFF(MINUTE, entered_at, exited_at)): for an open visit exited_at is NULL, the TIMESTAMPDIFF is NULL, and SQL's AVG skips NULLs — so open visits count toward the total but not toward the average, with no extra filter. Results are grouped into the two reporting categories; a category with no visits in the window is zero-filled so the DTO always returns both.

      Parameters:
      from - inclusive range start, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      to - inclusive range end, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      parkId - a specific park, or null for the whole region (all parks)
      Returns:
      the populated VisitsReportDTO, or null if the query fails

    • cancellations

      public CancellationsReportDTO cancellations(String from, String to, Integer parkId)
      Cancellations report: a per-day breakdown of cancelled and no-show reservations over a date range, optionally narrowed to a single park, with range totals and a daily average.

      The source is the reservation table filtered to rows whose status is CANCELLED or NO_SHOW and whose status_changed_at (the moment the cancellation/no-show was recorded) falls within [from, to] inclusive. Rows are grouped by DATE(status_changed_at) and the two statuses are split into separate counts per day; only days with activity produce a row.

      avgPerDay divides the grand total (cancelled + no-show) by the number of calendar days the inclusive range spans — DAYS.between(from, to) + 1, floored at 1 to avoid division by zero on a degenerate range. Quiet days therefore count as zero in the denominator, giving the average daily load across the whole window rather than only across the days that happened to have cancellations.

      Parameters:
      from - inclusive range start, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      to - inclusive range end, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      parkId - a specific park, or null for the whole region (all parks)
      Returns:
      the populated CancellationsReportDTO, or null if the query fails

    • usage

      public UsageReportDTO usage(String from, String to, Integer parkId)
      Usage report: lists each park/date that had recorded visits but did not reach full capacity. This gives the department manager the required view of when a park was not fully occupied.
      Parameters:
      from - inclusive range start, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      to - inclusive range end, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      parkId - a specific park, or null for all parks
      Returns:
      the populated usage report, or null if the query fails

    • daysInRange

      private long daysInRange(String from, String to)
      Number of calendar days the inclusive range [from, to] spans, used as the avgPerDay denominator. from == to is one day; the result is floored at 1 so a malformed or reversed range never divides by zero.
      Parameters:
      from - inclusive range start, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      to - inclusive range end, ISO yyyy-MM-dd
      Returns:
      the number of days spanned, at least 1
